questions.question: Explain the Von Neumann architecture with its key characteristics.
keyPoints: Programs and data stored in same memory
Single bus for instructions and data
Components: CPU, Memory, I/O via bus

questions.question: Describe the three stages of the instruction cycle.
keyPoints: Fetch: Get instruction from memory to IR
Decode: Interpret instruction and generate control signals
Execute: Perform operation using ALU or move data

questions.question: What are the different types of I/O data transfer techniques? Compare them.
keyPoints: Interrupt-driven I/O: Device interrupts when ready
DMA: Direct transfer between I/O and memory without CPU

questions.question: Explain the concept of memory hierarchy and its purpose.
keyPoints: Layered structure: Registers → Cache → RAM → Secondary
Based on speed vs cost tradeoff
Purpose: balance speed, capacity, and cost

questions.question: Differentiate between RISC and CISC architectures.
keyPoints: RISC: Few simple instructions, fixed length, single cycle
CISC: Many complex instructions, variable length, multi-cycle

questions.question: Explain the role of different CPU registers.
What is an interrupt? Explain its processing sequence.
Draw and explain the functional block diagram of a computer system showing CPU, memory, and I/O organization.
keyPoints: Draw diagram showing CPU (ALU, CU, Registers) connected to system bus
Show memory module connected to bus
Show I/O controllers connected to bus
Explain address and control signal flow

questions.question: Explain the different addressing modes with examples.
Explain the organization of the CPU and the data path with the help of registers and ALU.
keyPoints: CPU components: Control Unit, ALU, Registers
Data path: MAR → Memory → MDR → IR → Decoder → CU → ALU
Role of accumulator in arithmetic operations

questions.question: Explain the concept of system bus and describe the functions of address, data, and control buses.
keyPoints: Data bus: Bidirectional, carries actual data
Address bus: Unidirectional, carries memory addresses
Control bus: Carries control signals (read/write, interrupt, clock)
Impact of bus width on performance
